name: azure-ad-integration
description: Microsoft Graph API for Azure AD / Entra ID. Manage users, groups, devices, and run security investigations: sign-in logs, risk detections, risky users, audit logs, Conditional Access, and incident response.

tenant_id.title: Tenant ID
tenant_id.description: Your Azure AD tenant ID (Directory ID). Find it in Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Overview.

client_id.title: Client (App) ID
client_id.description: App registration client ID. IMPORTANT: All permissions must be Application type (not Delegated) with admin consent granted — this app uses client credentials (no user sign-in). Microsoft Graph Application permissions needed: AuditLog.Read.All, Directory.Read.All, IdentityRiskyUser.ReadWrite.All, IdentityRiskEvent.ReadWrite.All, Mail.Read (Application — required to read any user's mailbox), MailboxSettings.Read, Policy.Read.All, Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess, ThreatHunting.Read.All, User.Read.All, User.RevokeSessions.All, UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All. Also needs Office 365 Management APIs: ActivityFeed.Read. NOTE: Mail.Read as Delegated (not Application) will only allow access to one mailbox and cause ErrorItemNotFound on all others.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: App registration client secret. Create one in Azure Portal → App registrations → Certificates & secrets.

tools.name: azure_ad_list_users
tools.description: List Azure AD users with optional filter, search, and field selection

tools.name: azure_ad_get_user
tools.description: Get a user by UPN or object ID

tools.name: azure_ad_search_users
tools.description: Search users by display name, UPN, or email

tools.name: azure_ad_user_member_of
tools.description: Get groups and directory roles a user belongs to

tools.name: azure_ad_user_manager
tools.description: Get a user's manager

tools.name: azure_ad_user_direct_reports
tools.description: Get a user's direct reports

tools.name: azure_ad_user_devices
tools.description: Get devices owned or registered by a user

tools.name: azure_ad_list_groups
tools.description: List Azure AD groups with optional filter

tools.name: azure_ad_get_group
tools.description: Get a group by name or object ID

tools.name: azure_ad_group_members
tools.description: List members of a group

tools.name: azure_ad_group_owners
tools.description: List owners of a group

tools.name: azure_ad_list_devices
tools.description: List Azure AD registered devices with optional filter

tools.name: azure_ad_get_device
tools.description: Get a device by display name or object ID

tools.name: azure_ad_organization
tools.description: Get tenant/organization information

tools.name: azure_ad_domains
tools.description: List verified domains in the tenant

tools.name: azure_ad_licenses
tools.description: List subscribed SKUs (license counts and consumption)

tools.name: azure_ad_directory_roles
tools.description: List active directory roles and their members

tools.name: azure_ad_sign_ins
tools.description: Query sign-in logs — filter by user, IP, app, error code, risk level, or time window. Essential for compromise investigation.

tools.name: azure_ad_sign_in_get
tools.description: Get full detail for a specific sign-in event by ID

tools.name: azure_ad_risk_detections
tools.description: Query identity risk detection events (leaked credentials, impossible travel, attacker IPs, etc.)

tools.name: azure_ad_risky_users
tools.description: List users currently flagged as risky by Identity Protection

tools.name: azure_ad_risky_user_history
tools.description: Get risk history for a specific user (risk level changes, detections)

tools.name: azure_ad_audit_logs
tools.description: Query directory audit logs — filter by user, activity, category, or time window

tools.name: azure_ad_auth_methods
tools.description: List authentication methods registered for a user (MFA methods, passkeys, etc.)

tools.name: azure_ad_named_locations
tools.description: List named locations (trusted IP ranges, country allow/deny lists)

tools.name: azure_ad_revoke_sessions
tools.description: Revoke all active sign-in sessions for a user (incident response). Requires confirm=True to actually revoke; returns preview of affected sessions when confirm=False.

tools.name: azure_ad_confirm_compromised
tools.description: Mark users as confirmed compromised in Identity Protection (triggers remediation). Requires confirm=True to execute; returns preview when confirm=False. Immediately locks the user account.

tools.name: azure_ad_sent_emails
tools.description: List emails sent from a user's mailbox — identifies phishing/spam sent via stolen tokens. Filter by time window.

tools.name: azure_ad_search_mail
tools.description: Search a mailbox folder by subject keyword or sender. Uses $filter (works on any mailbox with Mail.Read Application permission). Prefix query with 'from:email' to filter by sender. Supports hours parameter for time window.

tools.name: azure_ad_get_email
tools.description: Get full detail of a specific email message including complete body — drill into suspicious messages found via azure_ad_sent_emails

tools.name: azure_ad_ual_inbox_rules
tools.description: Query Unified Audit Log for inbox rule creation/modification events — forensic attribution with creator IP and session ID

tools.name: azure_ad_ual_mailbox_access
tools.description: Query Unified Audit Log for mailbox access events — shows if attacker read emails or accessed folders after token theft

tools.name: azure_ad_ual_search
tools.description: Search the Unified Audit Log for any Exchange or Azure AD operations by operation name, user, or time window

tools.name: azure_ad_list_ca_policies
tools.description: List all Conditional Access policies with state and conditions summary

tools.name: azure_ad_create_named_location
tools.description: Create an IP-based Named Location (trusted IP range) for use in CA policies

tools.name: azure_ad_create_ca_policy
tools.description: Create a Conditional Access policy — block or require MFA for specific users from non-trusted locations (incident response)

tools.name: azure_ad_update_ca_policy
tools.description: Enable, disable, or rename an existing Conditional Access policy

tools.name: azure_ad_delete_ca_policy
tools.description: Delete a Conditional Access policy. Requires confirm=True to execute; returns preview when confirm=False. This action is irreversible.

tools.name: azure_ad_advanced_hunt
tools.description: Run a KQL query against Microsoft 365 Defender Advanced Hunting (EmailEvents, IdentityLogonEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, etc). Captures events invisible to Graph API and UAL — including SaveToSentItems=false sends. Requires ThreatHunting.Read.All. Requires confirm=True to execute; returns preview dict with confirm=False (dry run by default).

tools.name: azure_ad_email_events
tools.description: Query EmailEvents via Defender Advanced Hunting — the ONLY API that captures emails sent with SaveToSentItems=false (attacker phishing blasts). Aggregates by NetworkMessageId to show full recipient lists. Requires ThreatHunting.Read.All.

tools.name: azure_ad_user_oauth_grants
tools.description: List OAuth delegated permission grants for a user — detects attacker persistence via OAuth app grants that survive password resets and session revocations. Returns app names, scopes, and expiry.

tools.name: azure_ad_mailbox_settings
tools.description: Get mailbox settings including SMTP forwarding address and auto-reply config — detects post-compromise exfiltration via forwarding rules set outside inbox rules (survives inbox rule cleanup).

tools.name: azure_ad_mfa_changes
tools.description: Query audit logs for MFA/authentication method changes — detects attacker adding their own phone or authenticator app to maintain access after password reset. Covers register/delete security info events.

tools.name: azure_ad_role_changes
tools.description: Query audit logs for role assignment changes — detect privilege escalation (Add/Remove member to/from role). Identifies if attacker added themselves or a backdoor account to Global Admin or other privileged roles.

tools.name: azure_ad_email_attachments
tools.description: Query EmailAttachmentInfo via Defender Advanced Hunting — identifies files sent by compromised accounts (file name, type, SHA256, malware family). Captures attachments from SaveToSentItems=false emails. Requires ThreatHunting.Read.All.

tools.name: azure_ad_ual_sharepoint
tools.description: Query UAL for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams activity — detects post-compromise data exfiltration: file downloads, anonymous link creation, external sharing, Teams access from attacker IPs.

tools.name: azure_ad_delete_inbox_rule
tools.description: Delete a specific inbox rule from a user mailbox (incident response remediation). Requires confirm=True to execute; returns preview when confirm=False. Use rule IDs from azure_ad_ual_inbox_rules or azure_ad_incident_triage.

tools.name: azure_ad_dismiss_risky_users
tools.description: Dismiss the risk state for one or more risky users in Identity Protection (call after remediation: password reset + sessions revoked + MFA re-enrolled). Requires confirm=True to execute; returns preview when confirm=False.

tools.name: azure_ad_incident_triage
tools.description: Full security triage for one or more compromised accounts — orchestrates sign-in analysis, inbox rule inspection (UAL-validated), sent mail forensics via EmailEvents (captures SaveToSentItems=false), mailbox forwarding check, OAuth grants check, auth methods, and UAL forensics in one call. Produces HIGH/MEDIUM/CLEAN risk summary per user.
